title: R2 Precision Decision I/O Contract (§2.1.1 a-stage)
description: Both shapes the _r2_decide_precision.py executor sees: Input (per-detector eval/timing/audit bundle the executor consumes) and Output (canonical entry shape in runs/_r2_precision_decisions.json). Field set + decision_case enum + sensitivity_sweep keys + threshold_pass dict are pinned at elegant-sauteeing-quail.md LOCK-iter-3. Input.class_wise items inline the §1.1 audit-coverage record fields (audit_coverage_status / audit_low_power / construction_failure_reason?) and reference _r2_audit_coverage_schema.json#/items via $ref so the cross-schema invariant is enforceable when a registry-aware validator is used; the test driver builds a referencing.Registry from both schemas to make the $ref load-bearing at validation time. Outputs of decide_precision MUST validate against #/definitions/PrecisionDecisionsArray; inputs the executor accepts MUST validate against #/definitions/PrecisionDecisionsInput.

ConfidenceInterval.description: Bootstrap CI tuple (1000 resamples, 95% interval per locked plan). Decision rule keys on ci_low for CI-aware comparisons. Numeric ordering ci_low <= point <= ci_high is NOT expressible in JSON Schema draft-07 (no cross-property numeric ordering operator); writers/verifiers MUST call _r2_schema_utils.enforce_ci_ordering(records, CI_FIELDS_PER_DECISION_OUTPUT_RECORD) after schema validation. The schema rejects malformed shapes (missing fields, non-number types); the runtime helper rejects malformed orderings (e.g. {point: -0.1, ci_low: 0.2, ci_high: 0.3}).

AuditFields.description: Reference to _r2_audit_coverage_schema.json per-record shape. The audit-coverage record's required fields (class_id / class_name / full_val_support / full_val_insufficient / audit_support / audit_coverage_status / audit_low_power) become the canonical audit fields inside ClassWiseDecisionItem; runtime registry resolution makes this $ref load-bearing.

name.description: Class name. Must match class_name in the corresponding _r2_audit_coverage.json record (writer-enforced cross-file invariant; _r2_verify.py asserts byte-equality on the audit-fields subset by class name).

delta_AP.description: FP32-FP16 per-class AP delta on the frozen R2 val set. Sign convention: positive = FP32 better.

support_tier.description: Reporting tier when full_val_support >= 30: 30-99=low_block, 100-499=medium_block, >=500=high_block. null when full_val_insufficient=true. Per locked plan: tier is reporting-only, does not change blocking rule. Cross-field consistency with full_val_support is enforced by allOf rules below.

allOf.description: audit_coverage_status pinned to audit_support range; mirrors _r2_audit_coverage_schema.json rule.
construction_failure_reason present iff status=construction_failed; mirrors audit schema rule.

rationale.description: Free-text but minLength enforced per locked plan: silent exclusion of pedestrian-signal classes (or any safety-set member) is forbidden.

worst_class_name.description: Empty when num_safety_classes_regressed=0 (cross-field rule below). Class name from R2 manifest otherwise.

allOf.description: When no safety class regressed, all worst-* fields must be zero/empty (no spurious worst-class data).

AllThresholdsPass.description: Per-threshold pass flags (one per swept parameter in locked plan). False = threshold tripped.

loc_regression_pp.description: Map from threshold value (string-encoded e.g. '-0.1') to ship_precision under that single-parameter override.

joint.description: Three named joint-profile bundles per locked plan.

detector.description: Closed enum locked to the four R2 candidates in elegant-sauteeing-quail.md LOCK-iter-3. Adding a candidate requires schema bump.

decision_case.description: 4 main cases (A=ship FP32, B=ship FP16 default, C=catch-all FP16 with regression flag, D=mixed-signal FP16) + 3 escapes (inconclusive_global / audit_disagreement / executor_error) per locked plan. Mutually exclusive — exactly one terminal outcome per detector.

selected_artifact_path.description: Repo-relative path to the selected TRT engine. Same canonicalization rules as _r2_component_decision_schema.json blocking_artifacts paths, plus required '.engine' suffix because precision decisions ALWAYS pick an engine artifact.

pre_committed_defer_outcome.description: Optional. Set when build-determinism check (§2.1.2 c) trips threshold and forces fp16-default per pre_committed_defer_outcome rule. Single-value enum so writers can't smuggle ad-hoc strings; new outcomes require schema bump.

allOf.description: Case C is the catch-all for blocked FP32; Cases A/B/D never trigger investigation_required. Per locked plan: only Case C requires investigation note.
Reverse direction: investigation_required=true → decision_case=C. Closes the smuggle channel where a writer could flag investigation on B/D outcomes.
Case A ships FP32; ship_precision must be fp32.

Cases B/C/D ship FP16 (locked plan §-Decision-rule). All escape cases also ship FP16: audit_disagreement (locked plan §-Audit-subset-action), inconclusive_global (locked plan §-Inconclusive-evidence-outcome), executor_error (defense-in-depth: a crash report MUST NOT default to FP32).
pre_committed_defer_outcome only valid on inconclusive_global escape (build variance defer flows through inconclusive_global per locked plan).

PrecisionDecisionsArray.description: Top-level shape for runs/_r2_precision_decisions.json. Sub-key uniqueness on `detector` is unenforceable in draft-07; writers/verifiers MUST call _r2_schema_utils.validate_and_enforce(records, schema_path, unique_keys=['detector']) before persisting/accepting. Output array MUST contain exactly 4 entries (one per R2 candidate) at R2 close per §4.1; this schema does NOT enforce minItems/maxItems=4 because the executor may write partial records during iteration — final-state count check lives in _r2_verify.py.

audit_coverage_path.description: Path to runs/_r2_audit_coverage.json. The hash-pin (audit_coverage_sha256) is the load-bearing field; path is for runtime location.

audit_coverage_sha256.description: SHA256 hash-pin of the audit-coverage JSON file. _r2_decide_precision.py MUST recompute the file's actual hash and assert equality before consuming any audit_records — per §1.1 b-stage propagation rule (low_power → confidence-downgrade; construction_failed → escape outcome).

audit_records.description: Inlined audit-coverage records (the $ref resolves to _r2_audit_coverage_schema.json#/items at validation time). Required by §2.1.1 a-stage spec: 'input 必含 §1.1 audit audit_coverage_status 字段 + $ref 到 §1.0 _r2_audit_coverage_schema.json'. Executor MUST cross-check audit_records against audit_coverage_path's actual file content and audit_coverage_sha256.

RepoRelativePath.description: Same canonicalization rules as _r2_component_decision_schema.json blocking_artifacts paths.
